title: Backfill Verification for Status Events

migrations.description: Initial schema: users, customers, profiles, requests, request_notes, request_invoices, request_images, device_tokens, OTP, service_types tables with enum types
Seed: 5 service_types (Electrical, Plumbing, HVAC, General Maintenance, Security) via ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE — idempotent
Creates request_status_events table; backfills legacy rows; adds indexes

backfill_analysis.sql:
insert into request_status_events (request_id, status, changed_by_profile_id, changed_at)
select r.id, r.status, r.customer_location_profile_id, coalesce(r.created_at, now())
from requests r
where not exists (
  select 1
  from request_status_events rse
  where rse.request_id = r.id
);

checks.detail: WHERE NOT EXISTS guard ensures no duplicate events if migration is re-run. Safe to replay.
Selects ALL requests without a pre-existing event. No request can be silently skipped unless the request row itself is corrupt.

Backfill records r.status at migration-run time (June 5 2026), not the original creation status. For requests that transitioned before the migration (e.g., pending_approval -> completed), the backfilled event captures the final status, not the chain. Functionally acceptable — the timeline endpoint is designed to show 'state at point in time' and newly created requests are fully accurate. Historical accuracy is bounded to pre-June-5 transitions.
Uses COALESCE(r.created_at, now()) — anchors the synthetic event at request creation time, not migration time. Correct.
Uses customer_location_profile_id as backfill author — the originating party for request creation. Semantically correct.
r.status is NOT NULL in schema (NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending_approval'); r.customer_location_profile_id is NOT NULL. No null propagation risk.
Migration adds idx_request_status_events_request_id and idx_request_status_events_changed_at. Both queries in the timeline endpoint are indexed.

forward_path_checks.detail: Request INSERT and initial status event INSERT are wrapped in BEGIN/COMMIT. If the event insert fails, the request is rolled back. No orphan requests can exist without an event.
Event is only inserted when currentStatus !== statusToSet. No duplicate events on idempotent PATCH calls. Event insert is inside BEGIN/COMMIT with the status update — atomic.

UNION ALL of request_status_events (event_type='status_change') and request_notes (event_type='note'). Both legs use occurred_at for ordering. Ownership check (profile ID match) prevents cross-tenant leakage. visibility filter on notes ('public' or matching profile_type) is correct.
When a contractor cancels/declines, status is reset to 'pending_approval' and contractor_profile_id is set to null. A status_change event is still recorded for the new effective status. Correct.

seed_checks.detail: 20260604190000_seed_service_types.sql seeds 5 records: Electrical, Plumbing, HVAC, General Maintenance, Security. ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE — idempotent.
Live DB row count not verified: platform API Supabase credentials not available in current environment. Expected: 5 rows. Run: SELECT count(*) FROM service_types;

Cannot verify live row count without DB access. Required check: SELECT count(*) FROM requests r WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM request_status_events WHERE request_id=r.id); Expected result: 0 (no unmatched requests).
Cannot verify timestamp ranges without DB access. Required check: SELECT count(*) FROM request_status_events WHERE changed_at > now(); Expected result: 0 (no future-dated events).

api_reachability_checks.detail: api.simplyclik.com did not respond (HTTP 000 / connection refused) from both remote server and local machine. May be VPN-only or not yet DNS-live. Cannot confirm live deployment status from current environment.
Same network constraint. Would return 200+service_types array if accessible.

verdict: Backfill SQL logic is correct: idempotent WHERE NOT EXISTS guard, proper timestamp anchoring (coalesce to request.created_at), correct author attribution. Forward path is fully correct: new requests and status changes both emit events atomically in transactions. Timeline endpoint queries the table correctly with proper ownership and visibility filtering. One acceptable historical limitation: backfill captures status at migration time, not full transition history for pre-existing requests — this is expected and not a correctness defect. 3 manual checks remain for live DB row-count and timestamp plausibility verification (no platform API Supabase credentials available in current environment). API endpoint unreachable from current network — VPN or internal access required for live smoke test.
required_manual_steps: Connect to platform API Supabase and run: SELECT count(*) FROM requests r WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM request_status_events WHERE request_id=r.id); — expect 0
Run: SELECT count(*) FROM request_status_events WHERE changed_at > now(); — expect 0
Run: SELECT count(*) FROM service_types; — expect 5
Run rollout-readiness.ts with valid ROLLOUT_BASE_URL/ROLLOUT_TOKEN once api.simplyclik.com is reachable
